message:
test(e2e): expand _diag-scroll-indicator to cover bakery/cmaes/hoeffding + sweep 320/360/375 viewports

The diagnostic spec used to iterate a flat list of article slugs at a
single 375x800 viewport and screenshot the hero, which was useful for
the slack/wills/raptorq/overprompting/barra set but didn't catch the
narrower-phone hero-overlap regressions the three math articles
(bakery_algorithm, cmaes_explainer, hoeffdings_d_explainer) were
hitting.

Restructure the fixture to:

- Associate each article with its exact "scroll to …" label, since the
  three math articles use different copy — "Discover", "Optimize", and
  "Initialization" respectively — so the spec can actually locate the
  indicator element instead of hoping for a generic selector.
- Add bakery_algorithm / cmaes_explainer / hoeffdings_d_explainer to
  the article list (each paired with its real label).
- Iterate three narrow viewports (320x568, 360x640, 375x667) per
  article instead of the single 375x800, because the overlap the fix
  targeted only reproduced below ~380px wide.

Test name rewritten from "mobile hero — {slug}" to
"no hero overlap — {slug} @ {viewport}" to reflect what it's actually
asserting. Screenshots go into /tmp/wills-audit with the viewport in
the filename so cross-width visual diffs are straightforward.
